name: The Carlyle, A Rosewood Hotel
character: 76th and Madison. The Carlyle is Manhattan's most literary, most musical, most quietly legendary hotel. Bemelmans Bar is the real New York. Café Carlyle has hosted Bobby Short for decades. The art deco suites have housed presidents, royalty, and artists without changing their character at all. The Carlyle is for guests who know New York and want to be inside it rather than near it.

cultural_calendar.name: Metropolitan Opera season
cultural_calendar.notes: The Carlyle is 20 minutes from Lincoln Center. Concierge maintains relationships with house managers for last-minute premium access.

cultural_calendar.name: Frieze New York
cultural_calendar.notes: Major art fair at the Shed. The hotel connects guests with gallery owners and collectors for private previews.

cultural_calendar.name: Central Park in autumn
cultural_calendar.notes: The Ramble is three blocks away. The concierge provides a printed map of the hidden garden paths known mostly to regulars.

signature_amenities: Personal shopping service with UES boutiques
Private rooftop access for long-stay guests
room_amenity_options: New York Times and International Herald Tribune delivered to door
Custom bar setup with guest's preferred spirits

welcome_amenity_options.name: Bemelmans signature cocktail in-room
welcome_amenity_options.description: The bar's house cocktail, prepared by Bemelmans' head bartender and delivered chilled.

welcome_amenity_options.name: New York reading selection
welcome_amenity_options.description: Books selected from The Strand or Argosy — fiction set in New York, chosen for this guest.

welcome_amenity_options.name: Seasonal Upper East Side flowers
welcome_amenity_options.description: From the florist on Lexington. Arranged in the room before arrival.
